sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
Approach turn 1 from the left and brake at about the 100m board, dropping down to 3rd gear. Its a sharp left-hand corner — hit a late apex and avoid over-committing. Turns 2 and 3 come quickly: a flowing right-left that rewards clean lines and throttle control. Stay tight, and dont overuse the inside kerbs— they can unsettle the car with relative ease if you attack them overzealously. Get a firm exit for the short burst into turn 4.

These corners are taken in 5th or 6th gear, and you need to be fully committed. Turn 4 is a flat-out left-hand bend, followed by a sweeping right in turn 5 and then left again for turn 6. Maintain a steady throttle, steer smoothly, and let the car flow. Dont clip the kerbs too hard here, they can compromise your balance heading into Sector 2.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Brake early and rotate the car smoothly. You will want to be in 3rd gear, clipping the inside kerb without over-rotating. Run wide and open the steering early to set up turn 8.

This is arguably the slowest and most awkward section of the track. Brake hard into turn 8, down to 2nd gear, and stay tight. Turn 9 comes immediately — flick the car left and try to straighten it out for the exit. Be cautious with the throttle; traction is limited, and the car can easily snap out from under you. You are right up against the walls in Miami, and mistakes are costly, so keep your concentration on the tarmac ahead of you at all times.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
Following a DRS straight, turn 11 is a heavy braking zone and a strong overtaking opportunity. Brake just after the 100m board, drop to 3rd gear, and aim for a late apex. Watch for lockups here. Get back on throttle smoothly — it sets up the next flowing corners.

These are high-speed direction changes taken in 4th or 5th gear. Focus on positioning — wide in, tight apex, then let the car run out. These are rhythm corners: overcorrection will cost you down the next straight.

This tight right-left leads into the final straight. Brake at the entry of turn 14, down to 3rd gear, and keep it tidy. Avoid overcommitting or bouncing off curbs. A clean exit is crucial to maintain speed on the DRS straight.

One last heavy braking zone. Down to 2nd gear, nail the apex, and prioritise exit speed. Short-shifting helps reduce wheelspin here, especially late in a race.
